businessSummary.oneLiner: Government and enterprise AI data analytics platform leader with Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI Platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%), with US commercial revenue surpassing $500M. The recent Keel integration into the Navy ShipOS project expanded defense AI into submarine production. The stock has risen 1,860% over 3 years while experiencing 10+ drops of 20% or more.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 10x+ the S&P 500 avg (22x). Extreme even for software avg (35x)

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS $1.34. Heavy growth premium but still demanding

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B, FY2026E $7.41B (+65.5%). Growth is actually accelerating

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS companies. High profitability alongside high growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF $2.1B. Best-in-class cash generation in software

growthDrivers.title: PLTR Rises While Market Crashes: Defense AI Independent Momentum
growthDrivers.description: S&P dropped 1.4% on Fed hawkishness while PLTR rose 1.9%. Defense AI has its own momentum that overcomes macro headwinds.

growthDrivers.title: UBS $200 Upgrade + Wedbush $230 Maintained
growthDrivers.description: UBS Karl Keirstead raised from $180 to $200 (+29%). Wedbush maintained $230 Buy. Wall Street bull case accelerating.

growthDrivers.title: NYT: 'Silicon Valley Bet on War. It's Paying Off'
growthDrivers.description: NYT spotlighted Palantir as the poster child of defense AI investment success. Defense tech going mainstream.

growthDrivers.title: Navy ShipOS $448M: AI Applied to Submarine Production
growthDrivers.description: Keel joined Palantir's Navy ShipOS project, applying AI to submarine production lines. Defense AI scope expanding.

growthDrivers.title: $11.2B Revenue Backlog + $10B Army Contract
growthDrivers.description: $10B Army contract reshaping defense software market. $11.2B backlog provides multi-year visibility.

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Outlook $7.41B (+65.5%), Growth Accelerating
growthDrivers.description: Growth rate accelerating from 56% to 65.5%. AI platform demand explosion.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ year track record in government/defense AI. Security clearance creates insurmountable entry barriers. ShipOS expands AI from battlefield to military production.

moats.description: Top-level clearance with DoD and intelligence agencies. Competitors cannot enter.
AI deployed in submarine production, naval shipyards. Expanding beyond battlefield to entire military supply chain.
Sovereign AI OS + NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Positioning for government AI infrastructure standard globally.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse. Expanding into analytics platform territory

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI. Trailing Palantir in scale and execution

recentNews.headline: PLTR Rises 1.9% Against Market as Fed Cuts Rate Outlook
recentNews.significance: S&P -1.4%, Nasdaq -1.4% while PLTR bucked the trend. Defense AI independent momentum confirmed.

recentNews.headline: UBS Raises Target from $180 to $200, Maintains Buy
recentNews.significance: Karl Keirstead sees +29% upside. Combined with Wedbush $230 (+48%), Wall Street bull case strengthens.

recentNews.headline: NYT: Silicon Valley Bet on War, It's Paying Off
recentNews.significance: Palantir spotlighted as defense AI success story. Mainstream media validates defense tech thesis.

recentNews.headline: UK Privacy Backlash Risks NHS Contract
recentNews.significance: Privacy concerns in UK threaten NHS contract. Government revenue concentration creates political vulnerability.

recentNews.headline: Keel Joins Palantir Navy ShipOS Project ($448M)
recentNews.significance: AI applied to submarine production. Defense AI scope maximized.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation at 226x P/E
risks.description: 10x the S&P 500 average. Fed rate cut reduction increases multiple compression risk. Though PLTR bucked the market today, maintaining defense AI premium.

risks.title: Continued Insider Selling
risks.description: CEO Alex Karp selling ~$6M daily. Management actively taking profits at current levels.

risks.title: Government Revenue Concentration + UK NHS Privacy
risks.description: UK privacy backlash threatens NHS contract. High government revenue dependence creates political vulnerability.

risks.title: Share Dilution
risks.description: ~5% annual dilution from stock-based compensation. Erodes per-share value over time.

risks.title: AI Market Competition
risks.description: Microsoft, AWS, Google investing heavily in AI platforms. Enterprise AI competition intensifying.

buyReasons.title: Only Stock Rising in Fed Crash: Defense AI Momentum
buyReasons.rationale: S&P -1.4% while PLTR +1.9%. Market-immune defense AI premium confirmed.

buyReasons.title: UBS $200 + Wedbush $230: Wall Street Target Rush
buyReasons.rationale: UBS upgraded to $200, Wedbush maintained $230. 16 of 28 analysts Buy.

buyReasons.title: $11.2B Backlog + 65.5% Growth Acceleration
buyReasons.rationale: Top-tier revenue visibility. Growth accelerating, not decelerating.

buyReasons.title: NYT Defense AI Spotlight: Mainstream Validation
buyReasons.rationale: Major media validates defense AI success thesis. Long-term positive for investor sentiment.

analystOpinions.notableComment: UBS $200 (+29%) upgrade, Wedbush $230 (+48%) maintained. 16 of 28 Buy. Defense AI premium confirmed as PLTR bucked the market selloff.

overallOpinion: While the Fed cut its rate outlook to 1 and the S&P dropped 1.4%, PLTR rose 1.9%. Defense AI has demonstrated independent momentum that overcomes macro headwinds.

UBS raised its target to $200 and NYT spotlighted Palantir as the defense AI success story. The $11.2B backlog and 65.5% growth acceleration provide the strongest fundamental backdrop in the company's history.

P/E 226x remains extreme and CEO Karp's daily $6M selling is a concern. Below the 50-day SMA ($158) but above the 200-day ($127), the long-term uptrend is intact. Buy on dips with dollar-cost averaging.

investmentVerdict.summary: PLTR proved its defense AI independent momentum by rising against a 1.4% market crash. UBS $200 upgrade, NYT spotlight, $11.2B backlog, and 65.5% growth acceleration make the fundamental case the strongest ever. P/E 226x is demanding but the stock has recovered from every 20%+ drawdown in 3 years. Buy on pullbacks with dollar-cost averaging.

threads.hook: PLTR rises while the market crashes on Fed
threads.text:
$PLTR $155.68 (+1.9%)

The Fed cut its 2026 rate outlook to just 1 cut and the S&P dropped 1.4%. PLTR was the rare stock that rose. Defense AI has its own momentum.

UBS raised to $200 (+29%), NYT spotlighted defense AI success, and the $11.2B backlog paired with 65.5% growth acceleration make the fundamental case the strongest ever.

P/E 226x is extreme and CEO Karp sells $6M daily. But this stock has recovered from every 20%+ drawdown in 3 years.

Below the 50-day but above the 200-day. Consensus $194 (+24.6%). Buy on pullbacks.

fact.text:
$PLTR $155.68 (+1.9%)

Fed held rates, signaled 1 cut. S&P -1.4%. PLTR rose against the market. UBS $200 (+29%) upgrade, Wedbush $230 (+48%) maintained. NYT defense AI spotlight.

$11.2B backlog, +65.5% growth. P/E 226x. Below 50-day, above 200-day, RSI 48.

16 of 28 Buy. Consensus $194 (+24.6%). Buy on pullbacks.

witty.hook: The market cried, PLTR went to war
witty.text:
$PLTR $155.68 (+1.9%)

Fed: "1 cut this year." Market: *crashes 1.4%* Palantir: *puts AI in submarines, goes up*

NYT wrote that Silicon Valley bet on war and it paid off. 1,860% in 3 years is indeed paying off. CEO Karp agrees, which is why he sells $6M worth every single day.

UBS raised to $200. Wedbush says $230. The $11.2B backlog and 65.5% growth acceleration make this the rare company where the bull case keeps getting stronger.

P/E 226x. Expensive? Obviously. Going higher? Historically, yes. $155.68, target $194 (+24.6%). Buy.

empathy.hook: For PLTR holders, today was a good day
empathy.text:
$PLTR $155.68 (+1.9%)

After a week of Fed anxiety, market crashes, and growth stock pain, today felt different. PLTR rose while everything else fell. Defense AI holders, you earned this.

UBS upgraded to $200. NYT validated the defense tech thesis. $11.2B backlog and 65.5% growth mean the fundamentals are the strongest they've ever been.

Yes, P/E 226x is extreme. Yes, Karp sells daily. But this stock has recovered from every 20%+ drop in 3 years. The pattern is your friend.

$155.68, target $194 (+24.6%). Buy on dips. The defense AI story isn't ending anytime soon.
